steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Factor $(x+5)$ from the left: $$\left(\tfrac{1}{4}-\tfrac{1}{3}\right)(x+5)=-7.$$ Since $\tfrac{1}{4}-\tfrac{1}{3}=-\tfrac{1}{12}$, this becomes $-\tfrac{1}{12}(x+5)=-7$.

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content: Multiply both sides by $-12$: $x+5=84$, so $x=79$. That matches choice C — C is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice C is correct. For the given equation, $\left(x+5\right)$ is a factor of both terms on the left-hand side. Therefore, the given equation can be rewritten as $\left(\frac{1}{4}-\frac{1}{3}\right)\left(x+5\right)=-7$, or $\left(\frac{3}{12}-\frac{4}{12}\right)\left(x+5\right)=-7$, which is equivalent to $-\frac{1}{12}\left(x+5\right)=-7$. Multiplying both sides of this equation by $-12$ yields $x+5=84$. Subtracting $5$ from both sides of this equation yields $x=79$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This is the value of $x$ for which the left-hand side of the given equation equals $\frac{7}{12}$, not $-7$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This is the value of $x$ for which the left-hand side of the given equation equals $0$, not $-7$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $x$ for which the left-hand side of the given equation equals $-\frac{209}{12}$, not $-7$.
